creator: fbsd_kstars_pr@proton.me
text:
The problem is on two machines, hpZbppk and hp dl380 gen 8 from a fresh install of Freebsd, uname -a:
FreeBSD plum 14.1-RELEASE FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE releng/14.1-n267679-10e31f0946d8 GENERIC amd64

Starting kstars brings up a dialog box:
  Failed to load the DSO database.
    Details:
      Can't prepare query!
      SQL ERROR: no such column: "%" - should this be a string literal in single-quotes? Unable to execute statement

The terminal shows:
Call to writableLocation without an application-based location.
org.kde.kstars: Welcome to KStars 3.6.6 Stable
org.kde.kstars: Build: 2024-10-19T01:28:22Z
org.kde.kstars: OS: "freebsd"
org.kde.kstars: API: "x86_64-little_endian-lp64"
org.kde.kstars: Arch: "x86_64"
org.kde.kstars: Kernel Type: "freebsd"
org.kde.kstars: Kernel Version: "14.1-RELEASE"
org.kde.kstars: Qt Version: 5.15.15
org.kde.kstars: Detected old format filter table, re-creating...
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "table effectivefov already exists")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: remotedrivers")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: Manufacturer")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: indihub")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: defectmap")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: portselector")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: gain")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: iso")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: scripts")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "table opticaltrains already exists")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "table profilesettings already exists")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "table opticaltrainsettings already exists")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "table dslrlens already exists")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: Train")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: FocalReducer")
org.kde.kstars: QSqlError("1", "Unable to execute statement", "duplicate column name: FocalRatio")
org.kde.kstars: Processing  "unnamedstars.dat" , HTMesh Level 3
org.kde.kstars:   Sky Mesh Size:  512
org.kde.kstars: Loaded DSO catalog file:  "unnamedstars.dat"
org.kde.kstars: "Star HD20,794 not found."
org.kde.kstars: "Star HD98,230 not found."
org.kde.kstars: Loading comets
KCrash: Application 'kstars' crashing...
QSocketNotifier: Invalid socket 7 and type 'Read', disabling...
KCrash: Attempting to start /usr/local/lib/libexec/drkonqi
kf.kirigami: Failed to find a Kirigami platform plugin
QStandardPaths: XDG_RUNTIME_DIR not set, defaulting to '/tmp/runtime-peter'
file:///usr/local/lib/qt5/qml/org/kde/kirigami.2/ContextDrawer.qml:135:9: QML ListView: Binding loop detected for property "topMargin"

Suspended (signal)


clicking OK on the "Failed to load the DSO database" dialog box brings up another dialog box
  KStars - The KDE Crash Handler (on plum)
with the following backtrace:
(lldb) process attach --pid 1878
Process 1878 stopped
